_comment: Hand-tunable parameters for trajectory_viz/trajectory_plot.py. Every layout/style number the plotter uses lives here (no hard-coded literals in the .py). Lengths suffixed _in are INCHES (the vertical flow reserves bounding boxes in inches, then the figure height = sum of all reserved heights, so elements never overlap/overflow); fractions are of the figure width/height; font sizes are points. Edit and re-run plot_trajectory.py; a per-test wrapper may point at an alternative file via trajectory_plot.set_params(path).

legend._comment: Single shared key (residual / eps_M / eps_C / the two y=1 reference lines), drawn once below the subtitle instead of repeating per stage box. Entries are added only for quantities the trace actually carries (eps_* only when probe-traced). Height auto-sizes to ceil(n_entries/ncol) rows.

threshold._comment: Reference lines on the trajectory panels. The ε panel is now PRIMARY (left axis): the scale-free gate ε_M ≤ tol_M AND ε_C ≤ tol_C is what the solver actually decides convergence on, so a cell with ε_M below tol_M is converged even if the (de-emphasised, right-axis) residual ‖R‖/tol still sits above its own y=1 line — which is the visual confusion this foregrounding resolves. tol_M_default/tol_C_default are FALLBACK thresholds drawn when a trace does not carry its own tol_M/tol_C; they mirror the solver config defaults eps_tol_momentum / eps_tol_mass (config/base_config.json). Newer traces emit tol_M/tol_C and the plotter prefers those. ylim_pad is the multiplicative (log-axis) margin so threshold lines stay visible; eps_min_decades enforces a minimum decade span so a barely-moving ε axis still reads on clean ticks.
